title: Turning the tide on typhoon havoc in the Philippines
content: Communities in Cagayan, Philippines use anticipatory action—moving boats, securing farms and assets—to reduce typhoon damage. With FAO support, early preparation boosts resilience and speeds recovery from severe storms.

title: Can animals sense earthquakes?
content: Animals often show unusual behaviour before earthquakes, possibly reacting to environmental changes like ions, vibrations or magnetic shifts, but evidence remains mixed and not yet reliable for prediction.

title: Community rapid assessment report: The impact of Cyclone Ditwah on persons with disabilities in Sri Lanka 2025-2026
content: This report presents a rapid assessment of the impacts of a cyclone on persons with disabilities (PwDs) in Sri Lanka, conducted by Yellow Dot and partners.

title: Yellow dot communinty (YDC)
content: Yellow Dot is a Sri Lankan community-based initiative focused on collective care, safe spaces, and advocacy for marginalized groups, including persons with disabilities.

title: Validating disaster and climate resilience: how to create a gold standard for resilience-measurement
content: As climate risks intensify, fostering community resilience has become a global priority but a fundamental question remains for practice and policy: how can resilience be defined, measured, and proven to inform implementation?

title: How reintroducing beavers is changing our landscape
content: Beavers are often described as natural engineers. Their dams, ponds and burrows reshape waterways in ways that slow the flow of water, store carbon and create new wetlands rich in wildlife.

title: Majority of UK households not signed up for flood alerts, despite growing impact of severe weather, says British Red Cross
content: More than two thirds (67%) of UK households said they were impacted by recent storms and floods since the start of 2026, with 12% reporting high or severe impact, according to research from the British Red Cross.

title: Research offers path forward for integrating flood modeling methods
content: Before rain begins to fall, scientists and engineers can predict where a storm might cause flooding thanks to advanced modeling and digital simulations that help guide billion-dollar decisions.

title: FAMU-FSU College of Engineering
content: The FAMU-FSU College of Engineering is the joint college of engineering of Florida A&M University and Florida State University, the only such joint college of its kind in the United States.

title: Call for Applications: 7th Batch of the IRDR Young Scientists Programme
content: The IRDR Young Scientists Programme is inviting early-career researchers to apply for its 7th batch, offering opportunities to join a global network and contribute to innovative disaster risk reduction research.

title: PROCULTHER-NET 2 Final virtual conference
content: The conference will bring together European and international experts to present the results of the PROCULTHER-NET project and discuss future strategies for strengthening cultural heritage protection within disaster risk management.

title: Participatory forensic investigations of disasters (PARFORIN): Including people in collective disaster risk analysis through social cartography
content: The Forensic Investigations of Disasters (FORIN) guide provides important approaches for research and extension purposes in order to understand and reduce disaster risks.

title: Typhoons: the hidden lifeline in a drying world
content: A research team led by Professor Jonghun Kam from POSTECH has revealed that typhoons are a critical factor in mitigating global droughts by simulating a scenario where typhoon-induced precipitation is removed.

title: When drought hits, do dams and groundwater soften the blow?
content: Droughts are the world’s most widespread natural disaster, and climate change is making them longer, more intense, and possibly more frequent. But how much do droughts actually hurt local economies and can water storage help cushion that blow?

title: Unplanned urbanisation fuels landslide disasters amid emerging rainfall trends
content: Researchers collaborated to assess the extent to which human-induced climate change altered the likelihood and intensity of the heavy rainfall event that caused the landslides.

title: Extreme heat has extreme effects – but some like it hot
content: A sweeping study of the 2021 heat wave reveals major ecological losses—but also surprising species that thrived, offering crucial insight into how climate extremes reshape ecosystems.

title: Reducing risk together: moving towards a more holistic approach to multi-hazard and multi-risk assessment and management
content: Moving towards a more holistic approach to disaster risk management, in which a multi-hazard and multi-risk approach is central, offers many opportunities to increase society's resilience.

title: A probabilistic performance-based framework for heat vulnerability and risk assessment of buildings
content: As climate-induced hazards increase rapidly, the built environment's limited preparedness highlights the urgent need to enhance resilience.

title: Better climate change adaptation through transformative innovation - enabling conditions and future perspectives
content: There is a growing scientific consensus that Climate Change Adaptation (CCA) is not being implemented at the scale, depth and speed needed to avoid dangerous climate-related risks.

title: The Lancet Global Health: Modelling suggests climate change could drive millions globally into physical inactivity by 2050 and be linked to an estimated half a million premature deaths
content: Climate change–driven heat could push millions into physical inactivity by 2050, causing up to 700,000 extra premature deaths yearly and billions in productivity losses, especially in hotter low‑ and middle‑income regions.

title: Eaton fire sent a pollution wave across Los Angeles
content: A USC Dornsife-led study found that the Eaton fire’s daily carbon monoxide emissions far exceeded L.A. County’s average daily emissions from all human activity.

title: Models warn Thwaites Glacier could rival entire Antarctic ice loss by 2067
content: Thwaites Glacier is already one of the fastest changing and most closely watched glaciers on Earth, losing ice more than five times faster than in the 1990s.

title: Guide on the role of supreme audit institutions toward achieving climate objectives
content: This publication outlines how supreme audit institutions in Central and West Asia can strengthen national responses to climate change by improving the transparency, accountability, and effectiveness of climate-related policies, finance, and programmes.

title: GFDRR country evaluation: Tajikistan
content: This evaluation assesses the Global Facility for Disaster Reduction and Recovery (GFDRR) support to Tajikistan over 2015-2024.

title: Dry winter, early blooms put Kashmir's orchards and farmers at risk
content: A dry winter and record‑warm temperatures in Kashmir caused early orchard blooms, threatening pollination and harvests. Officials warn of water shortages and advise farmers to shift from water‑intensive crops to more resilient varieties.

title: First Africa Climate–Health Desk launched by ACMAD, WHO and WMO
content: Africa’s first Climate–Health Desk launches to deliver climate‑based health intelligence, strengthen early warnings, and boost collaboration between meteorological and health sectors across the continent.

title: Flames of change: Children and youth at the forefront of tackling urban heat
content: This special report focuses on the important role that children and youth can play in tackling urban heat and the urban heat island effect. Both top-down and bottom-up approaches are highlighted to showcase comprehensive strategies in action.

title: Extreme climate events and transboundary effects of global crop trade
content: Global crop trade will sharply amplify countries’ exposure to extreme heat, wet, and dry events as climate change intensifies, with major exporters like Australia and Russia driving much of this transboundary climate risk.

title: Warming waters threaten seafood supply
content: Fish are evolving ever smaller in order to survive temperature increases, new research warns. It’s a biological shift that will rob billions of meals from those who rely on fish for protein.

title: Avalanche risks are rising - researchers say governance must rise with them
content: The findings of a new study shows governance and preparedness rather than hazard magnitude determine whether avalanches become mass-casualty events.

title: ‘A for alert’: Palghar schools adopt disaster awareness alphabet to teach safety early
content: The familiar “A for Apple” has just been replaced with “A for Alert” in government schools across Palghar, thanks to an innovative and deeply personal campaign by a district official.

title: Mid-Day Infomedia Ltd.
content: Mid-Day is Mumbai's homegrown newspaper and a long-running brand that is squarely focused on the city and its residents.

title: BSC study reveals that the warming of the North Atlantic contributed to intensifying the Valencia DANA storm
content: Warming in the Mediterranean and North Atlantic made the 2024 Valencia DANA far more intense, with rainfall up to 40% higher, highlighting the need for high‑resolution climate simulations.

title: Urban flooding: Constructing climate-resilient infrastructure
content: Urban flooding is rising across Europe, and traditional hazard maps aren’t enough; new ICARIA and CLIMEMPOWER models assess cascading impacts on infrastructure, mobility, safety, and economies to support climate‑resilient urban planning.

title: “GeoDRR–2026: Geography of Disaster in the 21st Century: Challenges, Preparedness and Adaptations”
content: One-Day International Conference on GeoDRR–2026 Geography of Disaster in the 21st Century: Challenges, Preparedness and Adaptations (Hybrid Mode)

title: Resilience World Nexus (RWN) Summit 2026
content: The Resilience World Nexus Summit 2026 brings together global leaders to explore how resilience can drive performance, manage risk, and support long-term sustainable growth.

title: Resilience World Nexus
content: The Resilience World Nexus (RWN) Summit convenes C-suite executives and senior leaders who recognise that resilience drives performance, protects value, and secures long-term growth.

title: Half of people living in flood-prone areas never discuss climate risks with anyone, new international study finds
content: A study of 1,600 coastal residents in the Netherlands, UK, and US finds half never discuss flood or climate risks. Conversations are rare, driven mostly by friends and family, with women and those with flood experience more likely to engage.

title: 15 years after the Great East Japan earthquake & tsunami
content: Fifteen years after the 2011 Tōhoku earthquake and tsunami, Japan’s recovery highlights the power of early warning, a strong culture of preparedness, and “build back better” reconstruction, shaping global disaster‑risk policy and regional cooperation.

title: How governments can help Indigenous communities disproportionately impacted by wildfires
content: Indigenous communities face disproportionate wildfire impacts due to limited resources, jurisdictional gaps, and evacuation challenges. The article urges long-term funding, better coordination, and stronger prevention and mitigation efforts.
